name: Human cord blood hematopoietic progenitor cell
description: Human cord blood hematopoietic progenitor cell is a cellular therapy that is intravenously administered in unrelated donor hematopoietic progenitor stem cell transplantation procedures in patients with disorders affecting the hematopoietic system that are inherited, acquired, or result from myeloablative treatment. The therapy consists of hematopoietic progenitor cells, monocytes, lymphocytes, and granulocytes from human cord blood. Active and viable hematopoietic progenitor cells express the cell surface marker CD34.
